name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a global money transfer service enabling customers to send and receive funds across 200+ countries via app, online, or in-person locations.
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established international money transfer provider that has moved billions of dollars and served millions of customers worldwide. The company operates a network of 2,000+ partner locations across more than 200 countries and territories, including major banks, national retailers, and local businesses, making it accessible for both sending and receiving funds globally.

MoneyGram offers multiple ways to send and receive money: customers can transfer funds via mobile app, online portal, or at physical partner locations. Recipients can pick up cash at partner locations, receive funds directly to bank accounts, transfer to debit cards, or use digital wallets depending on their location. The company emphasizes competitive exchange rates, low transfer fees, and fast processing times, with the ability to track transfers from initiation to completion.

The company distinguishes itself through its extensive global partner network reaching into over 200 countries and territories, making it one of the more widely accessible money transfer options. MoneyGram has invested in digital capabilities with high app store ratings (4.9 stars on App Store with 600K reviews, 4.6 on Google Play with 119K reviews), and emphasizes security through encryption, fraud protection tools, and advanced security measures. The platform is positioned for both emergency transfers and routine international remittances.

MoneyGram's primary strength is its unmatched physical presence globally combined with digital sending options. However, consumers should note that while the company advertises competitive rates, actual fees and exchange rates vary by corridor and transfer method. The service is most practical for those with access to partner locations or reliable internet connectivity, and rates should always be compared against competitors before sending, as MoneyGram's rates are not universally the lowest available.
address: 1133 Old County Rd, San Carlos, CA 94070

company_info.headquarters: 1133 Old County Rd, San Carlos, CA 94070

pros: Operates 2,000+ partner locations across 200+ countries and territories for pickup flexibility
Multiple sending methods: mobile app, online, and in-person at partner locations
Multiple receive options: cash pickup, direct bank deposit, debit card transfer, or digital wallet
High customer satisfaction with 4.9-star rating on App Store (600K reviews) and 4.6 on Google Play (119K reviews)
Real-time transfer tracking from initiation to completion
Fast processing with same-day or next-day availability to major corridors
Advanced security features including encryption and fraud protection tools
cons: Actual exchange rates and fees vary significantly by destination country and transfer method—not always the most competitive
Website does not clearly display rate or fee structures upfront, requiring sign-up or quote request for pricing transparency
Physical location dependency still limits convenience in some rural or underserved areas despite 2,000+ partner network
Transfer speed depends on recipient's location and selected receive method; some options may not be instant
Customer service quality and problem resolution timelines not addressed on marketing website
best_for: Individuals sending remittances to family members overseas, especially to Mexico, Philippines, and Central America
People without bank accounts who need to send cash internationally via partner location pickups
International travelers or expats managing finances across multiple countries
Anyone prioritizing choice of delivery method (cash, bank deposit, digital wallet) over lowest possible rates
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for individuals sending international remittances who value convenience and choice of delivery method over shopping for the absolute lowest rates. The critical caveat is that rates and fees are not transparent upfront and should be compared against competitors (Wise, OFX, Western Union) before committing, as MoneyGram's costs are market-dependent and not universally competitive.
services: International money transfers to 200+ countries and territories
In-person cash transfers at 2,000+ partner locations
Cash pickup at partner retail and bank locations
User profile creation and account management
